differences.title: 1. REACTIVITY MODEL
differences.leftDesc: Updates only changed DOM nodes directly, no VDOM overhead.
differences.rightDesc: Uses Virtual DOM and reactivity system for efficient updates.

differences.title: 2. COMPILATION
differences.leftDesc: Compiles JSX to efficient vanilla JS, minimal runtime.
differences.rightDesc: Combines runtime rendering with compiler optimizations.

differences.title: 3. PERFORMANCE
differences.leftDesc: Extremely fast, often benchmarks higher due to no VDOM.
differences.rightDesc: Fast and efficient with its optimized VDOM.

differences.title: 4. LEARNING CURVE
differences.leftDesc: Requires understanding fine-grained reactivity and JSX.
differences.rightDesc: Easier to pick up with clear directives and concepts.

differences.title: 5. ECOSYSTEM
differences.leftDesc: Smaller but rapidly expanding community and tooling.
differences.rightDesc: Large, well-established community, libraries, and tools.

differences.title: 6. STATE MANAGEMENT
differences.leftDesc: Native reactive primitives for efficient state management.
differences.rightDesc: Dedicated state management libraries for complex apps.

differences.title: 7. JSX VS TEMPLATES
differences.leftDesc: Uses JSX for component structure, familiar to React devs.
differences.rightDesc: Uses familiar HTML-like templates with special directives.
